name: Halley's Comet
summary: Halley's Comet is the best-known periodic comet, visible from Earth approximately every 75 years. Historical records of its appearances go back millennia; it was the first comet proven to be periodic.

name: Hale–Bopp
summary: Comet Hale–Bopp became spectacularly visible in 1997, one of the brightest comets of the 20th century. It has a very long orbital period and will not return for thousands of years.

name: 67P/Churyumov–Gerasimenko
summary: 67P is the comet visited by ESA's Rosetta mission, which studied its nucleus and coma in detail and deployed the Philae lander. It is a Jupiter-family periodic comet.

name: Encke
summary: Comet Encke has one of the shortest known orbital periods (~3.3 years). It is the source of the Taurid meteor stream and has been observed many times since ancient records.

name: Hyakutake
summary: Comet Hyakutake passed close to Earth in 1996 and produced a spectacular, long-tailed display. It is a long-period comet with an orbit that will not return in recorded history.
